mnemonics: E.D.R. (for Elements, Diagrams, Relationships) - Remember the three core components of any requirement model.
keyPoints: Requirements modeling creates abstract descriptions of a system's requirements, often using visual diagrams.
Common strategies include scenario-based (use cases), data (ERDs), flow-oriented (DFDs), and class-based (class diagrams) modeling.
The goal is to identify, clarify, and organize requirements to reduce ambiguity and serve as a foundation for design.
commonMistakes: Focusing on a single modeling perspective (e.g., only data) instead of creating a multi-faceted view.
Confusing analysis models (what the system should do) with design models (how it will do it).
Creating overly complex models that are difficult for stakeholders to understand and validate.
